subject: Re: Request for help in writing a brief report on Jeffrey  Epstein's support for your research
body:
what do you need?,,, 

----- Original Message ----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Monday, April 9, 2007 10:50:53 AM
Subject: FW: Request for help in writing a brief report on Jeffrey Epstein's support for your research


How should I respond?





Lesley Groff
Executive Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein


-----Original Message-----
From: Cindy Vallaro [mailto:cvallaro@receptor.med.harvard.edu] 
Sent: Friday, April 06, 2007 12:10 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: FW: Request for help in writing a brief report on Jeffrey
Epstein's support for your research
Importance: High

Hi Lesley,

I am just following up on an email that Dr. Church sent you in early
March.
I have not heard back from you and just wanted to make sure that you
received the email.  Please send me confirmation of receipt.

We are looking to set up a more formal arrangement for receipt of funds
for the PGP Project.  We received the first installment and need to know
how to set up for subsequent funds.

Please let us know if there is anything you need us to do on our end.

Thank you
Cindy Vallaro
Center Coordinator
Harvard Medical School
Tel: 617-432-1278
Fax: 617-432-6513

-----Original Message-----
From: George Church [mailto:gmc@harvard.edu]
Sent: Wednesday, March 14, 2007 9:01 PM
To: lesley@nysgllc.com
Cc: mmollinedo@genetics.med.harvard.edu; gmc@harvard.edu;
cvallaro@genetics.med.harvard.edu
Subject: FW: Request for help in writing a brief report on Jeffrey
Epstein's support for your research

We are very grateful for the first installment of this grant, and would
like to arrange for some of the subsequent funds.  Please contact Mayra
or Cindy to arrange the transfer.  I've attached the budget sheet. 

--George

Hi George.  Jeffrey's response to your email:

let's do it


Lesley Groff
Executive Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein



From: George Church [mailto:g1m1c1@receptor.med.harvard.edu]
Sent: Wednesday, October 11, 2006 7:25 AM
To: 'David Grosof'; lesley@nysgllc.com
Cc: (George) Church; cvallaro@genetics.med.harvard.edu; 'Mollinedo,
Mayra '
Subject: RE: Request for help in writing a brief report on Jeffrey
Epstein's support for your research

David & Lesley,

Hopefully the report turned out well.  I'd love to see a copy.

Following up on Jeffrey's consistent enthusiasm to fund four people to
work on the Personal Genome Project (PGP), I've attached a draft budget.
Our scientific goals for that budget are to sequence as much DNA as
possible from ten PGP volunteers' genomes (probably the most
interpretable 1%) and integrate that with a broad set of their physical
and physiological characteristics.  The data will be kept in a private
database accessible by approved scientists until we get more community
clarity on how to make the data public.  We have HMS-IRB and volunteers'
permission to make it fully public at any point. Additional details are
described in these pages:
http://arep.med.harvard.edu/PGP/Obvious.html
http://arep.med.harvard.edu/PGP/

We expect to publish at least one peer-reviewed paper by the end of the
first year and another by the end of the 3 year project, each of which
will acknowledge the support of the Foundation unless other arrangements
are requested.  We will also engage the scientific and broader
communities in educational outreach to inspire interest in practical and
creative applications of personal genomics (analogous to the new
personal computers in the 1980s) and progress in
anti-genetic-discrimination laws & practice.
Suggestions are welcome.  Please let us know if this plan is acceptable
and when/how the funds would be transferred.

Thanks,
--George

*** George M. Church**************************************************
Professor of Genetics, Harvard Medical School,
     MIT Health Sciences & Technology
     Director of the Lipper Center for Computational Genetics,
     MIT-Harvard DOE Genomes to Life Center,
     NIH Center for Excellence in Genomic Science
Mail: NRB room 238, 77 Avenue Louis Pasteur, Boston, MA 02115 USA
Administrative assistance   http://arep.med.harvard.edu/
(617) 432-1278 Cindy Vallaro (cvallaro@genetics.med.harvard.edu)
(617) 432-5742 Mayra Mollinedo (mmollinedo@genetics.med.harvard.edu)
(617) 432-7562 direct line & voice mail fax: (617) 432-6513
**********************************************************************

From:  "Lesley Groff" <lesley@nysgllc.com>
Subj:  Jeffrey Epstein
Date:  Wed Sep 27, 2006 9:06 am

Good Morning  George. Jeffrey will be coming up to Harvard on Monday
Oct. 2nd and wondered if you might have some time to get together?
Please let me know.

Thanks,  Lesley

Lesley Groff
Executive Assistant to Jeffrey  Epstein

>From: David Grosof [ mailto:grosof@sloan.mit.edu]
>Sent: Friday, September 15, 2006 1:44 AM
>To: g1m1c1@receptor.med.harvard.edu
>Subject: Request for help in writing a brief report on Jeffrey 
>Epstein's support for your research
>
>Dear Dr. Church,
>
>I am writing to ask your help in preparing a brief, two-page report by 
>the end of this month on how your work has been helped by support from 
>Jeffrey Epstein.  I left a message on your voicemail at 617-432-7562 
>earlier today.
>
>Purpose
>I am consulting to a charitable organization directed by Jeffrey 
>Epstein (the C.O.U.Q. Foundation).*
>


 
____________________________________________________________________________________

From: David Grosof [ mailto:grosof@sloan.mit.edu]
Sent: Friday, September 15, 2006 1:44 AM
To: g1m1c1@receptor.med.harvard.edu
Subject: Request for help in writing a brief report on Jeffrey
Epstein's support for your research

Dear Dr. Church,

I am writing to ask your help in preparing a brief, two-page report by
the end of this month on how your work has been helped by support from
Jeffrey Epstein. I left a message on your voicemail at 617-432-7562
earlier today.

Purpose
I am consulting to a charitable organization directed by Jeffrey
Epstein (the C.O.U.Q. Foundation).*
